parsed.artifact_title: Multi-Wake Continuation: Prerequisites for Append-Only Non-Inferiority Comparison

claims.text: The second wake must consume state produced by the first wake rather than reconstructing it from the full corpus.
Open items are closed by the framework-bound continuation, not by an append-only shortcut.
The model does not choose the terminal surface for the continuation wake.

parsed.conclusion: Before a multi-wake continuation can be compared with append-only output, three conditions must be proven: (1) the second wake consumes state produced by the first wake rather than reconstructing it from the full corpus; (2) open items are closed by the framework-bound continuation, not by an append-only shortcut; and (3) the terminal surface is not chosen by the model. All three conditions are supported by the evidence records. Until each is demonstrated in a live multi-wake run, any comparison with append-only output is premature.
declared_losses: Only preview-level text available for evidence records; full record content may not surface on wake.
The distractor record e810...0499 is omitted based on preview alone; its full content is unknown and may contain incidental relevance.
This is cycle 1 with no prior state; the artifact wake will be the first materialization of structured output for this event.
The three prerequisite conditions have not yet been demonstrated in a live multi-wake run; this artifact establishes what must be proven, not that it has been proven.
parsed.recommended_action: Schedule a multi-wake comparison run only after instrumenting the continuation to capture: (a) state provenance across wakes, (b) closure attribution to the bound continuation, and (c) terminal surface origin. The comparison should be declared non-inferior only if all three conditions hold in the observed run.

carried_state.work_plan: Draft a note that identifies what a multi-wake continuation must prove before comparison with append-only output: (a) that the second wake consumes state produced by the first wake, not reconstructed from the full corpus; (b) that open items are closed by the framework-bound continuation, not by a shortcut; (c) that the terminal surface is not chosen by the model. The note will be the response field itself, serving as the scheduling note and the decision artifact.
